title: Final consumption expenditure (% of GDP)
definition: Final consumption expenditure is expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. This indicator is expressed as a percentage of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) which is the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Somalia, Fed. Rep., Lesotho, West Bank and Gaza, while the lightest include Ireland, Gabon, Brunei Darussalam; that contrast is the fastest way to read the final consumption share pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Final consumption as a share of GDP helps distinguish domestic-demand economies from investment- or export-heavy ones.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Domestic savings can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Household consumption can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
External balance can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Economic-share maps are not the same as raw totals.
Small open economies can dominate percentage maps.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 promotion: consumption share adds an economy-structure map distinct from trade and investment.
Practice-only because the GDP accounting concept is less intuitive for new players.
